Findings.Title: Real-Time Protection
Findings.Evidence: Microsoft Defender Real-Time Protection is active.
Findings.Explanation: Baseline endpoint monitoring operational.

Findings.Title: Active Defender Exclusions Detected!
Findings.Explanation: Malware frequently adds C:, Temp, or PowerShell to Defender exclusions to evade detection!
Findings.Recommendation: Review all exclusions in Windows Security and remove any unauthorized paths or file extensions immediately!

Findings.Title: Recent Malware Detection History
Findings.Explanation: Microsoft Defender intercepted a malware execution attempt (e.g., TrojanDownloader:PowerShell/Rozena.A).
Findings.Recommendation: Verify in Protection History that the threat status is Quarantined or Removed, and perform an Offline Scan.

Findings.Explanation: Network boundary filtering active.

Findings.Title: Active Local Account (hero)
Findings.Explanation: Authorized local user account.

Findings.Title: Potentially Unwanted / Risky Software (AnyDesk)
Findings.Evidence: Publisher: AnyDesk Software GmbH | Version: ad 9.7.3 | Path: "D:\Software\AnyDesk"
Findings.Explanation: Software matches keywords associated with software cracks, KMS activators, or remote access tools (RATs). Cracks/KMS tools are the #1 vector for TrojanDownloader:PowerShell/Rozena.A!
Findings.Recommendation: Uninstall 'AnyDesk' immediately if unauthorized and scan system with Defender Offline.

Findings.Title: Potentially Unwanted / Risky Software (qBittorrent)
Findings.Evidence: Publisher: The qBittorrent project | Version: 5.2.1 | Path:
Findings.Explanation: Software matches keywords associated with software cracks, KMS activators, or remote access tools (RATs). Cracks/KMS tools are the #1 vector for TrojanDownloader:PowerShell/Rozena.A!
Findings.Recommendation: Uninstall 'qBittorrent' immediately if unauthorized and scan system with Defender Offline.

Findings.Title: User Account Control (UAC)
Findings.Explanation: Privilege escalation prompts active.

Findings.Title: BitLocker Drive Encryption (C:)
Findings.Evidence: Volume C: protection status inactive / unencrypted.
Findings.Explanation: Physical access to drive allows data extraction without credentials.
Findings.Recommendation: Enable BitLocker drive encryption on system volume.

Findings.Title: UEFI Secure Boot
Findings.Explanation: System is susceptible to boot-level rootkits and pre-OS tampering.
Findings.Recommendation: Enable Secure Boot in motherboard BIOS/UEFI settings.

Findings.Title: PowerShell ScriptBlock Logging
Findings.Explanation: Obfuscated or memory-only PowerShell attacks cannot be forensically reconstructed from logs.
Findings.Recommendation: Enable PowerShell ScriptBlock Logging via Group Policy or Registry.
